commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `hooks.allowedAgentIds` evaluate the effective Gateway hook target agent, including omitted or blank `agentId` default routing, and updates related tests, docs, and config help.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +85, Docs 0, Generated 0. Total +98 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than a live run: current main allows omitted or blank `agentId` before checking `hooks.allowedAgentIds`, and the handler dispatches those requests through default-agent routing. The PR's focused tests cover that path.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the PR is protected, security-sensitive, compatibility-changing, and missing real behavior proof rather than needing a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(hooks): enforce default hook agent allowlist
target.body:
## Summary

Hardens Gateway hook agent routing for https://github.com/NVIDIA-dev/openclaw-tracking/issues/685 and GHSA-724r-v4wf-mqc5.

When `hooks.allowedAgentIds` is configured, omitted or blank `agentId` now checks the effective default agent before dispatch instead of bypassing the allowlist.

## Changes

- Enforce `hooks.allowedAgentIds` against the effective default agent when hook callers omit or blank `agentId`.
- Add helper and Gateway server regressions for omitted, blank, default-allowed, wildcard, empty allowlist, explicit, unknown fallback, and mapped hook routing cases.
- Align config type/help text, security audit remediation copy, docs, and the generated config baseline hash with the effective-agent semantics.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/security/audit-hooks-routing.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check src/gateway/hooks.ts src/gateway/hooks.test.ts src/gateway/server.hooks.test.ts src/config/types.hooks.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/security/audit-extra.sync.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/automation/cron-jobs.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
- `pnpm lint:docs docs/automation/cron-jobs.md docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean, no accepted/actionable findings)

## Notes

AI-assisted: yes, Codex.

Real behavior proof:

Behavior addressed: Hook agent allowlists now apply to the effective target agent, including default-agent routing when `agentId` is omitted or blank.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout at PR head `1a1496e945a3` with real `openclaw gateway run` processes on loopback, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and live `curl` requests to `/hooks/agent`. Config A used agents `main` (default) and `hooks` with `hooks.allowedAgentIds: ["hooks"]`; Config B used the same agents with `hooks.allowedAgentIds: ["main"]`. The hook bearer token was redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the restrictive Gateway with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=<tmp>/restrictive.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state-restrictive OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 corepack pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured --auth none --bind loopback --port 18798 --force`, sent the three redacted curl requests shown below, stopped that Gateway, then started the default-allowed Gateway with the same command shape on port `18799` and sent the omitted-`agentId` curl request shown below.

Evidence after fix:

```text
gateway restrictive ready: port=18798 sha=1a1496e945a3
case: restrictive allowlist, omitted agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof omitted","deliver":false}
status: 400
response: {"ok":false,"error":"agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds"}
case: restrictive allowlist, blank agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof blank","agentId":"   ","deliver":false}
status: 400
response: {"ok":false,"error":"agentId is not allowed by hooks.allowedAgentIds"}
case: restrictive allowlist, explicit allowed hooks agent
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof allowed","agentId":"hooks","sessionKey":"hook:explicit","deliver":false}
status: 200
response: {"ok":true,"runId":"7f86bba0-e7b5-447b-a6d7-2fb011cba50d"}
gateway default-allowed ready: port=18799 sha=1a1496e945a3
case: default-agent allowlist, omitted agentId
request: POST /hooks/agent {"message":"codex proof default allowed","deliver":false}
status: 200
response: {"ok":true,"runId":"538f275f-f661-4b1b-918f-23d6ed9712d4"}
```

Observed result after fix: Omitted and blank `agentId` are denied with HTTP 400 when the effective default agent is not allowlisted; an explicit allowlisted `hooks` agent route is accepted with HTTP 200 and a real `runId`; omitted `agentId` is accepted with HTTP 200 and a real `runId` when the default agent `main` is allowlisted.

What was not tested: No public webhook deployment, full repository Vitest suite, or real model completion was run. The accepted hook routes used `deliver:false`; the proof targets the Gateway hook routing/allowlist behavior ClawSweeper requested.
target.author: eleqtrizit
